MatchCriteria.description: Criteria for matching Python symbols. Fields at the same level are combined with AND logic (all must match). Use 'any_of' for OR logic and 'all_of' for explicit AND grouping. Criteria objects may be nested recursively.

all_of.description: Match if ALL of the listed sub-criteria match (AND logic). Can be nested inside 'any_of' for complex boolean expressions.

any_of.description: Match if ANY of the listed sub-criteria match (OR logic). Can be nested inside 'all_of' for complex boolean expressions.

member_type.description:
Match symbols of the specified AST-detected member type.
  'class'      – class definitions
  'function'   – function and async function definitions
  'variable'   – module-level variable assignments
  'constant'   – SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE module-level assignments
  'type_alias' – Python 3.12+ 'type X = ...' statements and pre-3.12 TypeAlias annotations
  'imported'   – symbols imported from another module
  'unknown'    – member type could not be determined

module_exact.description: Match symbols defined in a module whose dotted path is exactly this string (e.g. 'mypackage.core.__init__' or '__init__' for the root init).

module_pattern.description: Match symbols defined in a module whose dotted path matches this regular expression (Python re syntax, full-string match). Example: '.*\\.exceptions$'

name_exact.description: Match symbols whose __name__ is exactly this string.

name_pattern.description: Match symbols whose __name__ matches this regular expression (Python re syntax, full-string match).

provenance.description:
Match symbols by how they were introduced into the current module.
  'defined_here'   – defined directly in this file
  'imported'       – imported from another module (plain import)
  'alias_imported' – imported with an alias (import X as Y)
  'unknown'        – provenance could not be determined

PackageOverride.description: Per-package configuration override. Applied to the named package and all of its subpackages unless a more-specific override exists.

output_style.description: Output style for this package and its subpackages.

Rule.description: A single export rule. Rules are evaluated highest-priority-first; ties are broken alphabetically by name.

action.description:
Action to take when this rule matches.
  'include'     – mark the symbol for export
  'exclude'     – suppress the symbol from export
  'no_decision' – pass through without a decision (useful as a fallback/catch-all)

description.description: Human-readable description of what this rule does (documentation only).

match.description: Criteria that must be satisfied for this rule to apply to a symbol.

name.description: Unique rule name. Used as a tie-breaker when two rules share the same priority (alphabetically earlier name wins).

priority.description:
Evaluation priority in the range 0–1000 (higher = evaluated first). Default: 500.

Recommended bands:
  1000      – absolute exclusions (private members, dunders)
  900–800   – infrastructure/framework exclusions
  700       – primary export rules (classes, functions, constants)
  600–500   – import handling
  300–400   – special cases / propagation overrides
  0–200     – defaults and fallbacks

propagate.description:
How far to propagate an included symbol up the package hierarchy.
  'none'   – do not propagate beyond the module's own __init__.py
  'parent' – propagate one level up to the immediate parent package
  'root'   – propagate all the way to the top-level package __init__.py
  'custom' – reserved for future use

Only meaningful when action is 'include'. Has no effect on 'exclude' or 'no_decision' actions.

description: Schema for exportify configuration files (.exportify/config.yaml and project rule files).

metadata.description: Optional informational metadata. Keys and values are arbitrary strings and are not interpreted by exportify.

output_style.description: Default output style for all generated __init__.py files. 'lazy' (default) generates a __getattr__-based lazy import pattern; 'barrel' generates direct star-import re-exports.

overrides.description: Per-package output style overrides. Keys are dotted module paths (e.g. 'mypackage.compat'). The most-specific matching ancestor wins; falls back to the top-level output_style.

project.description: Project identification settings. Not required but encouraged. If not provided, exportify attempts to identify these values from various sources, which may be unreliable and is not deterministic (i.e. may change based on environment or exportify version). Providing explicit values here ensures consistent project identification regardless of environment or exportify version.

additional_source_paths.description: Additional relative paths from the project root (or absolute paths) to source directories. Useful when your project has multiple source roots (e.g. typical python monorepo with 'src' and 'packages'). These paths are considered in addition to the main source_path when resolving modules for export rules.

project_name.description: Project name. Used for cache namespacing and in generated SPDX headers when enabled. If not provided, exportify attempts to infer the project name from pyproject.toml or the project root directory name.

project_path.description: Path to the project root directory. Used for cache namespacing and as the base path for module resolution. If not provided, exportify attempts to auto-detect the project root by looking for common Python project files (e.g. pyproject.toml, setup.py) or falling back to the current working directory.

source_path.description: Relative path from the project root to the source root directory containing Python packages. Used as the base path for module resolution. If not provided, exportify attempts to auto-detect the source root by trying to find and inspect the pyproject.toml or looking for common source conventions (specifically 'src' or a directory with the same name as the parent directory like 'mypackage/mypackage').

rules.description: Export rules evaluated in priority order (highest priority first). The first rule whose 'match' criteria are satisfied determines the export decision. If no rule matches, the symbol receives no_decision and is not exported.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this configuration file. Must be '1.0'.

spdx.description: SPDX license header settings for files generated by exportify. When enabled, each generated __init__.py will include SPDX comment headers at the top.

copyright.description: SPDX-FileCopyrightText value, e.g. '2026 Acme Corp.' or '2026 Acme Corp. <legal@acme.com>'.

enabled.description: Whether to prepend SPDX comment headers to generated __init__.py files. Default: false.

license.description: SPDX-License-Identifier value, e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', or 'MIT OR Apache-2.0'.

title: Exportify Configuration
